client_updates.highlight: Zeam: DevNet 4 stable for 64 nodes except intermittent snappy P2P encoding error; DevNet 5 interop begun with Ethlambda
Qlean: DevNet 5 image ready; interop confirmed with Ethlambda locally; snappy decoding issue under investigation for DevNet 4
Grandine: Updated tag, profiler started, lean spec PRs 781 and 802 implemented; DevNet 5 testing locally

testing_progress.highlight: Snappy decode error on DevNet 4 gossip is non-deterministic; Lantern suspected as source; all teams asked to run isolated two-client tests
Consensus reached to move focus to DevNet 5 rather than wait for full DevNet 4 stabilization
DevNet 5 hardware baseline: 8-core/16 GB per node appears sufficient for regular validators; aggregator nodes may need more
leanMultisig commit hashes: DevNet 4 = f66d4a9 (~10–15% perf gain); DevNet 5 = 8fcbd77; teams must align on correct commit per devnet

documentation.highlight: Eth Research post published: 'Exploring the Design Space for a Post-Quantum Public Key Registry for Ethereum Validators' — covers XMSS, Poseidon, KoalaBear, registry design, open questions; intended as EIP discussion starter
lean spec refactor in progress: abbreviations removed, concerns split, folders reorganized, test coverage increased; targeting internal EF presentation by end of month

leanvm_updates.highlight: LeanVM: Rayon parallelization library being replaced with custom thread pool to stabilize ZK async memory usage and fix architecture-dependent instabilities

action_items.action: Each team run isolated two-client DevNet 4 tests to confirm whether Lantern is sole source of snappy error
Teams align on correct leanMultisig commit hash per devnet (DevNet 4: f66d4a9, DevNet 5: 8fcbd77) — resolve async in group chat
Review and comment on Eth Research post on PQ public key registry

decisions.decision: Move primary DevNet testing focus to DevNet 5; Zeam main branch moving to DevNet 5 spec immediately

targets.target: Compile DevNet 4 report with performance metrics before fully closing it out
lean spec refactor complete for internal EF presentation by end of June
